fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Univalence is easily established using the univalence of each \mathcal {B} x.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Display of left-cloven families of path objects

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {A} be a path object. Any left-cloven family of path objects \mathcal {B} \colon \mathopen {} \left \lvert \mathcal {A} \right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {PathObj} gives rise to a canonical displayed path object over \mathcal {A} in which:

fr:ol.fr:li: for p \colon x \approx _{ \mathcal {A} } y, we define b \approx _{ \mathcal {B} }^{ p } b' to be p_*b \approx _{ \mathcal {B} y } b';

for x: \mathopen {} \left \lvert \mathcal {A} \right \rvert \mathclose {} and b: \mathopen {} \left \lvert \mathcal {B} x \right \rvert \mathclose {}, the identity element \mathsf {rx}_{ \mathcal {B} } {x} \colon b \approx _{ \mathcal {B} }^{ \mathsf {rx}_{ \mathcal {A} } {x} } b is given by the component \Phi _{ \mathcal {B} } xb \colon \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf {rx}_{ \mathcal {A} } {x} \right ) \mathclose {} _*b \approx _{ \mathcal {B} x } {b} of the unit law of the given right cleaving of \mathcal {B}.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Univalence is easily established using the univalence of each \mathcal {B} x.
